message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We introduce an explicit function that describes virus-load curves on a patient-specific level. This function is based on simple and intuitive model parameters. It allows virus load analysis without solving a full virus load dynamic model. We validate our model on data from influenza A as well as SARS-CoV-2 infection data for Macaque monkeys and humans. Further, we compare the virus load function to an established
                  <jats:italic>target model</jats:italic>
                  of virus dynamics, which shows an excellent fit. Our virus-load function offers a new way to analyse patient virus load data, and it can be used as input to higher level models for the physiological effects of a virus infection, for models of tissue damage, and to estimate patient risks.
                </jats:p>
title: Personalized Virus Load Curves of SARS-CoV-2 Infection

reference.article-title: SARS-CoV-2 infection protects against rechallenge in rhesus macaques
